agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design isolates each external system behind adapter boundaries, routes all domain events through an internal event bus, and uses outbound projectors to transform and deliver data in each system's required protocol and format. This keeps the core domain model clean while handling three radically different integration styles.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Core Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the canonical domain model and emits all domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Enforces business invariants. This is the single source of truth for order and shipment lifecycle.","boundaries":"No direct knowledge of external systems. Communicates only via domain events published to the Internal Event Bus. Never imports XML schemas, carrier DTOs, or Kafka serialization concerns."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"Durable, ordered event backbone (e.g., internal Kafka topics or a lightweight broker like RabbitMQ). All domain events flow through here. Provides at-least-once delivery guarantees and consumer group isolation so each integration adapter processes independently.","boundaries":"Accepts only canonical domain events. No external system formats leak into event payloads. Partitioned by aggregate ID (orderId) to guarantee per-order ordering."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A)","responsibility":"Outbound ACL. Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events from the Internal Event Bus. Transforms canonical events into SAP-specific XML documents conforming to SAP IDoc/BAPI schemas. Batches and writes XML files to the SFTP drop directory on a 15-minute schedule. Handles SFTP connection management, retry on upload failure, and idempotency via deduplication keys embedded in filenames.","boundaries":"Isolated module. Owns the XML schema mapping and SFTP client. No SAP concepts leak back into the domain. If SAP changes its XML schema, only this adapter changes."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B)","responsibility":"Bidirectional ACL. OUTBOUND: Subscribes to ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments, mapping canonical ShipmentCreated to DHL's booking request DTO. INBOUND: Exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks (picked up, in transit, delivered, failed). Translates DHL-specific statuses into domain commands (ConfirmDeliveryAttempt, ConfirmDeliveryCompleted, ConfirmDeliveryFailed) and submits them to the Core Domain Service.","boundaries":"Owns DHL DTO mappings, authentication (API keys, OAuth), and webhook signature verification. The webhook endpoint must respond within 500ms — it acknowledges immediately and processes asynchronously. DHL's domain language (waybill, tracking events) is translated at this boundary and never enters the core."},{"name":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","responsibility":"Decouples webhook receipt from processing. The webhook endpoint writes the raw callback payload to a fast durable queue (e.g., SQS or internal Kafka topic) and returns 200 OK immediately (sub-500ms). A separate consumer reads from this queue, applies the ACL translation, and issues commands to the Core Domain Service.","boundaries":"Stores raw DHL payloads temporarily. Provides retry and dead-letter semantics for failed translations or transient core service errors."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Projector (System C)","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL domain events from the Internal Event Bus. Transforms each event into the analytics platform's expected schema (likely Avro or JSON with a specific envelope). Produces messages to the external Kafka cluster/topics that the Analytics Platform consumes. Handles schema registry integration if Avro is used.","boundaries":"Owns the mapping from canonical domain events to analytics event schema. Publishes to external Kafka topics distinct from the internal event bus topics. If analytics schema changes, only this projector changes."},{"name":"Integration Health Monitor","responsibility":"Monitors health of all three integration channels. Tracks: SFTP upload success/failure rates, DHL API latency and error rates, webhook ingestion lag, analytics Kafka producer lag. Emits alerts on SLA breaches (e.g., SFTP batch missed its 15-min window, webhook queue depth growing, analytics lag exceeding threshold).","boundaries":"Read-only observer. Does not participate in the data flow. Consumes metrics from adapters and queues."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Core Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes domain events: {eventId, eventType, aggregateId, timestamp, payload}. Events are immutable and versioned (v1, v2 schema evolution).","coupling_type":"loose — publish-subscribe, core has no knowledge of consumers"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events. Consumer group: sap-erp-adapter. Processes at own pace with offset tracking.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus, no coupling to producer"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Subscribes to ShipmentCreated events. Consumer group: dhl-carrier-adapter.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"DHL REST API","contract":"HTTPS POST to DHL booking endpoint. Request: DHL-specific booking DTO (JSON). Response: tracking number, waybill ID. Auth: OAuth2 client credentials.","coupling_type":"tight — synchronous HTTP call to external API, mitigated by circuit breaker and retry"},{"from":"DHL Webhook Endpoint","to":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","contract":"Raw DHL callback JSON enqueued with metadata (receivedAt, idempotencyKey derived from DHL's event ID).","coupling_type":"loose — fire-and-forget into queue"},{"from":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","to":"Core Domain Service","contract":"Consumer translates DHL payload into domain commands (ConfirmDeliveryAttempt, ConfirmDeliveryCompleted, ConfirmDeliveryFailed) and calls Core Domain Service via internal API/direct invocation.","coupling_type":"moderate — command invocation, but decoupled from webhook timing"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Stream Projector","contract":"Subscribes to ALL event types. Consumer group: analytics-projector.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Projector","to":"External Kafka (Analytics Platform)","contract":"Produces to analytics Kafka topics (orders.events, deliveries.events). Schema: Avro with schema registry or JSON with versioned envelope.","coupling_type":"moderate — schema contract with external team, mitigated by schema registry"}],"data_flow":"1. Core Domain Service processes business logic and publishes domain events to Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP Adapter consumes OrderConfirmed → batches XML → SFTP upload every 15 min. 3. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated → calls DHL REST API to book. DHL later sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Endpoint enqueues to Ingestion Queue (sub-500ms ack) → Queue Consumer translates to domain commands → Core Domain Service processes → emits DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed events back to bus. 4. Analytics Projector consumes ALL events → transforms → publishes to external Kafka topics in real-time. 5. All flows are independent — failure in one integration does not cascade to others.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model, protocol, and language. ACLs at each boundary prevent SAP XML schemas, DHL DTOs, and analytics Avro schemas from polluting the core domain model. When any external system changes, only its adapter changes."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Internal Event Bus","rationale":"All six domain events flow through a central bus, enabling each integration to consume independently at its own pace. This decouples the core from all three systems and allows adding new integrations (System D, E, ...) without modifying existing components."},{"pattern":"Async Webhook Ingestion (Claim Check variant)","rationale":"DHL webhooks have a 500ms SLA. The webhook endpoint immediately enqueues and acknowledges, then a background consumer processes at its own pace. This guarantees SLA compliance regardless of downstream processing time."},{"pattern":"Outbox / Batch Projector (for SAP)","rationale":"SAP expects batched XML files every 15 minutes, not real-time events. The SAP adapter accumulates confirmed orders and flushes on a schedule, matching SAP's consumption pattern without forcing the core into batch thinking."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker (for DHL REST API)","rationale":"The DHL API is a synchronous external dependency. A circuit breaker prevents cascading failures when DHL is degraded — shipment creation attempts are queued for retry rather than failing the entire flow."},{"pattern":"Schema Registry / Versioned Events","rationale":"The analytics platform and internal consumers need to evolve independently. Versioned event schemas with backward compatibility rules allow the core to evolve events without breaking downstream consumers."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: placed → confirmed → shipped → delivered/failed, invariants: cannot confirm without valid items, cannot ship without confirmation)"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: shipmentId, linked to orderId, states: created → carrier_booked → in_transit → delivered/failed, invariants: one active shipment per order, delivery status transitions are valid FSM)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["SapExportBatch (root: batchId, accumulates confirmed orders, tracks export status: pending → exported → acknowledged)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking (root: bookingId, maps shipmentId to carrier-specific tracking info, manages webhook event deduplication via carrier event IDs)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["No aggregates — stateless projector. Transforms and forwards events."]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes OrderConfirmed events. ERP Integration consumes and adapts to SAP format. Order Management is unaware of ERP needs."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier with ACL. Shipment Management publishes ShipmentCreated. Carrier Integration translates to DHL booking. Inbound: Carrier Integration acts as upstream for delivery status, feeding commands back into Shipment Management through an ACL."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Published Language. Both core contexts publish domain events in a canonical schema. Analytics Integration consumes all events via the published language and translates to analytics format."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Single Internal Event Bus as backbone rather than point-to-point integrations","gains":"Complete decoupling of integrations, easy to add new consumers, natural audit log of all events, independent scaling per consumer","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of running and monitoring a broker, eventual consistency between core and integrations, added latency (milliseconds) vs direct calls","alternatives_considered":["Point-to-point adapters called directly from domain service — simpler initially but creates tight coupling and N×M integration complexity","Shared database polling — avoids broker but introduces coupling through shared state and polling lag"]},{"decision":"Async webhook ingestion via queue rather than synchronous processing","gains":"Guaranteed sub-500ms webhook response regardless of processing complexity, retry semantics for free, dead-letter queue for poison messages","sacrifices":"Slight delay between webhook receipt and domain state update, additional infrastructure component (queue)","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous webhook processing — simpler but risks 500ms SLA breach under load or when core service is slow","Webhook processing with timeout-and-retry — fragile, DHL may not retry reliably"]},{"decision":"Batch XML generation for SAP on 15-minute schedule rather than real-time file drops","gains":"Matches SAP's consumption cadence exactly, reduces SFTP connection overhead, allows batch-level idempotency and error handling","sacrifices":"Up to 15-minute delay between order confirmation and SAP awareness, batch failures affect multiple orders","alternatives_considered":["Real-time individual XML file per order — excessive SFTP overhead, SAP may not process fast enough","SAP API integration instead of SFTP — would require SAP to expose APIs, unlikely given the SFTP requirement"]},{"decision":"Separate internal event bus topics from external analytics Kafka topics","gains":"Internal schema can evolve freely, analytics schema is a separate contract, security boundary between internal and external Kafka","sacrifices":"Duplication of event data across two Kafka clusters/topic sets, analytics projector is an additional moving part","alternatives_considered":["Direct consumption by analytics platform from internal topics — simpler but leaks internal schema and creates tight coupling, security risk of external system reading internal bus"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new integrations (System D, E, ...)","evolution_path":"New integrations only need a new consumer on the Internal Event Bus and a new ACL adapter. Zero changes to the core domain or existing adapters."},{"area":"Changing carrier from DHL to FedEx/UPS","evolution_path":"Implement a new Carrier Adapter behind the same ACL interface. The Carrier Integration bounded context abstracts carrier-specific details. Core domain emits the same ShipmentCreated events regardless of carrier."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Versioned events (v1, v2) with schema registry allow backward-compatible changes. New fields can be added without breaking existing consumers. Breaking changes use new event versions with a migration window."},{"area":"SAP replacement or modernization","evolution_path":"If SAP is replaced with a modern ERP with REST APIs, only the SAP Adapter changes — swap SFTP/XML for REST/JSON. The OrderConfirmed subscription and ACL boundary remain."},{"area":"Scaling webhook processing independently","evolution_path":"The DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue allows scaling webhook consumers horizontally independent of the webhook endpoint and the core domain service."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema is the central contract","reason":"All three integrations depend on the canonical domain event schema. Changes to event structure require coordinating across adapters. This is intentional rigidity — the canonical model should be stable and well-governed."},{"area":"Event ordering per aggregate","reason":"Internal Event Bus must guarantee per-orderId ordering. This constrains partitioning strategy and broker choice. Relaxing this would risk out-of-order processing (e.g., DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated)."},{"area":"Webhook endpoint availability","reason":"DHL will send webhooks regardless of our readiness. The endpoint must be highly available — it is a synchronous contract with an external system. Downtime means lost delivery status updates unless DHL retries."}],"risks":[{"risk":"DHL webhook delivery failures or retries with duplicate events could cause double-processing of delivery status updates","severity":"high","mitigation":"Idempotency keys derived from DHL's native event IDs stored in CarrierBooking aggregate. Deduplication at the ingestion queue consumer level. Dead-letter queue for unprocessable webhooks."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP batch failure (network, auth, disk full) causes a backlog of undelivered orders to ERP","severity":"high","mitigation":"SapExportBatch aggregate tracks export status. Failed batches are retried with exponential backoff. Alert on consecutive failures. Manual re-export capability via admin tooling."},{"risk":"Internal Event Bus outage halts all three integrations simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy event bus as a highly available cluster (e.g., Kafka with replication factor 3, min ISR 2). Monitor broker health aggressively. Consider a local outbox table in the core database as a fallback — events are first written to the outbox transactionally, then relayed to the bus (Transactional Outbox pattern)."},{"risk":"Analytics Kafka producer lag causes backpressure or data staleness in real-time analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer lag. Analytics projector runs in its own consumer group with independent scaling. Backpressure does not affect other integrations due to consumer group isolation."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal domain events and external system expectations","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward or full compatibility mode). CI pipeline validates that adapter transformations handle current event schema versions. Contract tests per adapter."},{"risk":"DHL API outage prevents shipment booking, blocking the fulfillment flow","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker on DHL API calls. Failed bookings are queued for retry (ShipmentCreated events remain on the bus with offset uncommitted or retried via a retry topic). Alert operations team for manual intervention if circuit stays open beyond threshold."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement incrementally, one integration at a time. Start with the Internal Event Bus and Core Domain events. Then add the Analytics Projector (simplest — stateless, read-only). Then the SAP Adapter (outbound only, batch schedule). Finally the DHL Adapter (most complex — bidirectional, real-time, webhook SLA). Each integration is independently deployable and testable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/core/domain/events/ — OrderPlaced.ts, OrderConfirmed.ts, ShipmentCreated.ts, DeliveryAttempted.ts, DeliveryCompleted.ts, DeliveryFailed.ts","src/core/domain/aggregates/ — Order.ts, Shipment.ts","src/infrastructure/eventbus/ — EventBusProducer.ts, EventBusConsumer.ts, EventSerializer.ts","src/integrations/sap/ — SapAdapter.ts, SapXmlMapper.ts, SftpClient.ts, SapExportBatchRepository.ts, SapBatchScheduler.ts","src/integrations/dhl/ — DhlAdapter.ts, DhlApiClient.ts, DhlDtoMapper.ts, DhlWebhookController.ts, DhlWebhookConsumer.ts, CarrierBookingRepository.ts","src/integrations/analytics/ — AnalyticsProjector.ts, AnalyticsEventMapper.ts, ExternalKafkaProducer.ts","src/infrastructure/monitoring/ — IntegrationHealthMonitor.ts","tests/integration/ — sap-adapter.integration.test.ts, dhl-adapter.integration.test.ts, analytics-projector.integration.test.ts","tests/contract/ — dhl-webhook.contract.test.ts, sap-xml.contract.test.ts, analytics-schema.contract.test.ts"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters) — core domain defines ports (event publisher interface, carrier booking port, ERP export port). Each integration is an adapter implementing those ports. This makes the core testable in isolation with mock adapters, and each adapter testable with mock event sources.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not let DHL DTOs, SAP XML types, or Kafka Avro schemas leak into the core domain model — keep ACLs strict","Do not process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always enqueue first, process second","Do not assume DHL webhooks arrive in order — a DeliveryFailed callback might arrive before DeliveryAttempted due to network timing. The Shipment aggregate FSM must handle out-of-order carrier events gracefully","Do not couple the 15-minute SAP batch schedule to a cron job alone — use a scheduler with distributed locking (e.g., ShedLock) to prevent duplicate batches in a multi-instance deployment","Do not share Kafka consumer groups across integrations — each adapter must have its own consumer group to process events independently","Do not skip the Transactional Outbox pattern for domain event publishing — without it, a crash between domain state commit and event publish causes lost events"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL pattern per integration — this is textbook and well-suited to three systems with radically different protocols","Async webhook ingestion to meet 500ms SLA — industry-standard approach","Event-driven backbone for decoupling integrations — well-proven pattern for multi-system integration","Batch projector for SAP SFTP — matches the stated 15-minute cadence requirement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific SAP IDoc/XML schema requirements — would need SAP team input to finalize the XML mapping","DHL API authentication and webhook signature verification specifics — depends on DHL's actual API documentation","Analytics platform's exact Kafka topic structure and schema requirements — need analytics team input","Whether the internal event bus should be Kafka, RabbitMQ, or another technology — depends on existing infrastructure and operational maturity"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform is a single deployable unit (monolith or modular monolith), not already a microservices architecture","Kafka is available or can be provisioned for the internal event bus","DHL's webhook contract includes an idempotency key or unique event ID","SAP expects standard IDoc-format XML files on SFTP, not a custom schema","The analytics platform can consume from Kafka topics directly","Event ordering per order is required (no out-of-order processing across integrations)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design isolates each external system behind adapter boundaries, routes all domain events through an internal event bus, and uses outbound projectors to transform and deliver data in each system's required protocol and format. content.thinking: Design an integration architecture for a logistics platform with three external system integrations.

webhook processing — simpler but risks 500ms SLA breach under load or when core service is slow","Webhook processing with timeout-and-retry — fragile, DHL may not retry reliably"]},{"decision":"Batch XML generation for SAP on 15-minute schedule rather than real-time file drops","gains":"Matches SAP's consumption cadence exactly, reduces SFTP connection overhead, allows batch-level idempotency and error handling","sacrifices":"Up to 15-minute delay between order confirmation and SAP awareness, batch failures affect multiple orders","alternatives_considered":["Real-time individual XML file per order — excessive SFTP overhead, SAP may not process fast enough","SAP API integration instead of SFTP — would require SAP to expose APIs, unlikely given the SFTP requirement"]},{"decision":"Separate internal event bus topics from external analytics Kafka topics","gains":"Internal schema can evolve freely, analytics schema is a separate contract, security boundary between internal and external Kafka","sacrifices":"Duplication of event data across two Kafka clusters/topic sets, analytics projector is an additional moving part","alternatives_considered":["Direct consumption by analytics platform from internal topics — simpler but leaks internal schema and creates tight coupling, security risk of external system reading internal bus"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new integrations (System D, E, ...)","evolution_path":"New integrations only need a new consumer on the Internal Event Bus and a new ACL adapter. Zero changes to the core domain or existing adapters."},{"area":"Changing carrier from DHL to FedEx/UPS","evolution_path":"Implement a new Carrier Adapter behind the same ACL interface. The Carrier Integration bounded context abstracts carrier-specific details. Core domain emits the same ShipmentCreated events regardless of carrier."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Versioned events (v1, v2) with schema registry allow backward-compatible changes. New fields can be added without breaking existing consumers. Breaking changes use new event versions with a migration window."},{"area":"SAP replacement or modernization","evolution_path":"If SAP is replaced with a modern ERP with REST APIs, only the SAP Adapter changes — swap SFTP/XML for REST/JSON. The OrderConfirmed subscription and ACL boundary remain."},{"area":"Scaling webhook processing independently","evolution_path":"The DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue allows scaling webhook consumers horizontally independent of the webhook endpoint and the core domain service."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema is the central contract","reason":"All three integrations depend on the canonical domain event schema. Changes to event structure require coordinating across adapters. This is intentional rigidity — the canonical model should be stable and well-governed."},{"area":"Event ordering per aggregate","reason":"Internal Event Bus must guarantee per-orderId ordering. This constrains partitioning strategy and broker choice. Relaxing this would risk out-of-order processing (e.g., DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated)."},{"area":"Webhook endpoint availability","reason":"DHL will send webhooks regardless of our readiness. The endpoint must be highly available — it is a synchronous contract with an external system. Downtime means lost delivery status updates unless DHL retries."}],"risks":[{"risk":"DHL webhook delivery failures or retries with duplicate events could cause double-processing of delivery status updates","severity":"high","mitigation":"Idempotency keys derived from DHL's native event IDs stored in CarrierBooking aggregate. Deduplication at the ingestion queue consumer level. Dead-letter queue for unprocessable webhooks."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP batch failure (network, auth, disk full) causes a backlog of undelivered orders to ERP","severity":"high","mitigation":"SapExportBatch aggregate tracks export status. Failed batches are retried with exponential backoff. Alert on consecutive failures. Manual re-export capability via admin tooling."},{"risk":"Internal Event Bus outage halts all three integrations simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy event bus as a highly available cluster (e.g., Kafka with replication factor 3, min ISR 2). Monitor broker health aggressively. Consider a local outbox table in the core database as a fallback — events are first written to the outbox transactionally, then relayed to the bus (Transactional Outbox pattern)."},{"risk":"Analytics Kafka producer lag causes backpressure or data staleness in real-time analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer lag. Analytics projector runs in its own consumer group with independent scaling. Backpressure does not affect other integrations due to consumer group isolation."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal domain events and external system expectations","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward or full compatibility mode). CI pipeline validates that adapter transformations handle current event schema versions. Contract tests per adapter."},{"risk":"DHL API outage prevents shipment booking, blocking the fulfillment flow","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker on DHL API calls. Failed bookings are queued for retry (ShipmentCreated events remain on the bus with offset uncommitted or retried via a retry topic). Alert operations team for manual intervention if circuit stays open beyond threshold."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement incrementally, one integration at a time. Start with the Internal Event Bus and Core Domain events. Then add the Analytics Projector (simplest — stateless, read-only). Then the SAP Adapter (outbound only, batch schedule). Finally the DHL Adapter (most complex — bidirectional, real-time, webhook SLA). Each integration is independently deployable and testable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/core/domain/events/ — OrderPlaced.ts, OrderConfirmed.ts, ShipmentCreated.ts, DeliveryAttempted.ts, DeliveryCompleted.ts, DeliveryFailed.ts","src/core/domain/aggregates/ — Order.ts, Shipment.ts","src/infrastructure/eventbus/ — EventBusProducer.ts, EventBusConsumer.ts, EventSerializer.ts","src/integrations/sap/ — SapAdapter.ts, SapXmlMapper.ts, SftpClient.ts, SapExportBatchRepository.ts, SapBatchScheduler.ts","src/integrations/dhl/ — DhlAdapter.ts, DhlApiClient.ts, DhlDtoMapper.ts, DhlWebhookController.ts, DhlWebhookConsumer.ts, CarrierBookingRepository.ts","src/integrations/analytics/ — AnalyticsProjector.ts, AnalyticsEventMapper.ts, ExternalKafkaProducer.ts","src/infrastructure/monitoring/ — IntegrationHealthMonitor.ts","tests/integration/ — sap-adapter.integration.test.ts, dhl-adapter.integration.test.ts, analytics-projector.integration.test.ts","tests/contract/ — dhl-webhook.contract.test.ts, sap-xml.contract.test.ts, analytics-schema.contract.test.ts"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters) — core domain defines ports (event publisher interface, carrier booking port, ERP export port). Each integration is an adapter implementing those ports. This makes the core testable in isolation with mock adapters, and each adapter testable with mock event sources.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not let DHL DTOs, SAP XML types, or Kafka Avro schemas leak into the core domain model — keep ACLs strict","Do not process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always enqueue first, process second","Do not assume DHL webhooks arrive in order — a DeliveryFailed callback might arrive before DeliveryAttempted due to network timing. The Shipment aggregate FSM must handle out-of-order carrier events gracefully","Do not couple the 15-minute SAP batch schedule to a cron job alone — use a scheduler with distributed locking (e.g., ShedLock) to prevent duplicate batches in a multi-instance deployment","Do not share Kafka consumer groups across integrations — each adapter must have its own consumer group to process events independently","Do not skip the Transactional Outbox pattern for domain event publishing — without it, a crash between domain state commit and event publish causes lost events"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL pattern per integration — this is textbook and well-suited to three systems with radically different protocols","Async webhook ingestion to meet 500ms SLA — industry-standard approach","Event-driven backbone for decoupling integrations — well-proven pattern for multi-system integration","Batch projector for SAP SFTP — matches the stated 15-minute cadence requirement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific SAP IDoc/XML schema requirements — would need SAP team input to finalize the XML mapping","DHL API authentication and webhook signature verification specifics — depends on DHL's actual API documentation","Analytics platform's exact Kafka topic structure and schema requirements — need analytics team input","Whether the internal event bus should be Kafka, RabbitMQ, or another technology — depends on existing infrastructure and operational maturity"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform is a single deployable unit (monolith or modular monolith), not already a microservices architecture","Kafka is available or can be provisioned for the internal event bus","DHL's webhook contract includes an idempotency key or unique event ID","SAP expects standard IDoc-format XML files on SFTP, not a custom schema","The analytics platform can consume from Kafka topics directly","Event ordering per order is required (no out-of-order processing across integrations)"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design isolates each external system behind adapter boundaries, routes all domain events through an internal event bus, and uses outbound projectors to transform and deliver data in each system's required protocol and format. This keeps the core domain model clean while handling three radically different integration styles.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Core Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the canonical domain model and emits all domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Enforces business invariants. This is the single source of truth for order and shipment lifecycle.","boundaries":"No direct knowledge of external systems. Communicates only via domain events published to the Internal Event Bus. Never imports XML schemas, carrier DTOs, or Kafka serialization concerns."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"Durable, ordered event backbone (e.g., internal Kafka topics or a lightweight broker like RabbitMQ). All domain events flow through here. Provides at-least-once delivery guarantees and consumer group isolation so each integration adapter processes independently.","boundaries":"Accepts only canonical domain events. No external system formats leak into event payloads. Partitioned by aggregate ID (orderId) to guarantee per-order ordering."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A)","responsibility":"Outbound ACL. Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events from the Internal Event Bus. Transforms canonical events into SAP-specific XML documents conforming to SAP IDoc/BAPI schemas. Batches and writes XML files to the SFTP drop directory on a 15-minute schedule. Handles SFTP connection management, retry on upload failure, and idempotency via deduplication keys embedded in filenames.","boundaries":"Isolated module. Owns the XML schema mapping and SFTP client. No SAP concepts leak back into the domain. If SAP changes its XML schema, only this adapter changes."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B)","responsibility":"Bidirectional ACL. OUTBOUND: Subscribes to ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments, mapping canonical ShipmentCreated to DHL's booking request DTO. INBOUND: Exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks (picked up, in transit, delivered, failed). Translates DHL-specific statuses into domain commands (ConfirmDeliveryAttempt, ConfirmDeliveryCompleted, ConfirmDeliveryFailed) and submits them to the Core Domain Service.","boundaries":"Owns DHL DTO mappings, authentication (API keys, OAuth), and webhook signature verification. The webhook endpoint must respond within 500ms — it acknowledges immediately and processes asynchronously. DHL's domain language (waybill, tracking events) is translated at this boundary and never enters the core."},{"name":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","responsibility":"Decouples webhook receipt from processing. The webhook endpoint writes the raw callback payload to a fast durable queue (e.g., SQS or internal Kafka topic) and returns 200 OK immediately (sub-500ms). A separate consumer reads from this queue, applies the ACL translation, and issues commands to the Core Domain Service.","boundaries":"Stores raw DHL payloads temporarily. Provides retry and dead-letter semantics for failed translations or transient core service errors."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Projector (System C)","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL domain events from the Internal Event Bus. Transforms each event into the analytics platform's expected schema (likely Avro or JSON with a specific envelope). Produces messages to the external Kafka cluster/topics that the Analytics Platform consumes. Handles schema registry integration if Avro is used.","boundaries":"Owns the mapping from canonical domain events to analytics event schema. Publishes to external Kafka topics distinct from the internal event bus topics. If analytics schema changes, only this projector changes."},{"name":"Integration Health Monitor","responsibility":"Monitors health of all three integration channels. Tracks: SFTP upload success/failure rates, DHL API latency and error rates, webhook ingestion lag, analytics Kafka producer lag. Emits alerts on SLA breaches (e.g., SFTP batch missed its 15-min window, webhook queue depth growing, analytics lag exceeding threshold).","boundaries":"Read-only observer. Does not participate in the data flow. Consumes metrics from adapters and queues."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Core Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes domain events: {eventId, eventType, aggregateId, timestamp, payload}. Events are immutable and versioned (v1, v2 schema evolution).","coupling_type":"loose — publish-subscribe, core has no knowledge of consumers"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events. Consumer group: sap-erp-adapter. Processes at own pace with offset tracking.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus, no coupling to producer"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Subscribes to ShipmentCreated events. Consumer group: dhl-carrier-adapter.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"DHL REST API","contract":"HTTPS POST to DHL booking endpoint. Request: DHL-specific booking DTO (JSON). Response: tracking number, waybill ID. Auth: OAuth2 client credentials.","coupling_type":"tight — synchronous HTTP call to external API, mitigated by circuit breaker and retry"},{"from":"DHL Webhook Endpoint","to":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","contract":"Raw DHL callback JSON enqueued with metadata (receivedAt, idempotencyKey derived from DHL's event ID).","coupling_type":"loose — fire-and-forget into queue"},{"from":"DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue","to":"Core Domain Service","contract":"Consumer translates DHL payload into domain commands (ConfirmDeliveryAttempt, ConfirmDeliveryCompleted, ConfirmDeliveryFailed) and calls Core Domain Service via internal API/direct invocation.","coupling_type":"moderate — command invocation, but decoupled from webhook timing"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Stream Projector","contract":"Subscribes to ALL event types. Consumer group: analytics-projector.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer pulls from bus"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Projector","to":"External Kafka (Analytics Platform)","contract":"Produces to analytics Kafka topics (orders.events, deliveries.events). Schema: Avro with schema registry or JSON with versioned envelope.","coupling_type":"moderate — schema contract with external team, mitigated by schema registry"}],"data_flow":"1. Core Domain Service processes business logic and publishes domain events to Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP Adapter consumes OrderConfirmed → batches XML → SFTP upload every 15 min. 3. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated → calls DHL REST API to book. DHL later sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Endpoint enqueues to Ingestion Queue (sub-500ms ack) → Queue Consumer translates to domain commands → Core Domain Service processes → emits DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed events back to bus. 4. Analytics Projector consumes ALL events → transforms → publishes to external Kafka topics in real-time. 5. All flows are independent — failure in one integration does not cascade to others.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model, protocol, and language. ACLs at each boundary prevent SAP XML schemas, DHL DTOs, and analytics Avro schemas from polluting the core domain model. When any external system changes, only its adapter changes."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Internal Event Bus","rationale":"All six domain events flow through a central bus, enabling each integration to consume independently at its own pace. This decouples the core from all three systems and allows adding new integrations (System D, E, ...) without modifying existing components."},{"pattern":"Async Webhook Ingestion (Claim Check variant)","rationale":"DHL webhooks have a 500ms SLA. The webhook endpoint immediately enqueues and acknowledges, then a background consumer processes at its own pace. This guarantees SLA compliance regardless of downstream processing time."},{"pattern":"Outbox / Batch Projector (for SAP)","rationale":"SAP expects batched XML files every 15 minutes, not real-time events. The SAP adapter accumulates confirmed orders and flushes on a schedule, matching SAP's consumption pattern without forcing the core into batch thinking."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker (for DHL REST API)","rationale":"The DHL API is a synchronous external dependency. A circuit breaker prevents cascading failures when DHL is degraded — shipment creation attempts are queued for retry rather than failing the entire flow."},{"pattern":"Schema Registry / Versioned Events","rationale":"The analytics platform and internal consumers need to evolve independently. Versioned event schemas with backward compatibility rules allow the core to evolve events without breaking downstream consumers."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: placed → confirmed → shipped → delivered/failed, invariants: cannot confirm without valid items, cannot ship without confirmation)"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: shipmentId, linked to orderId, states: created → carrier_booked → in_transit → delivered/failed, invariants: one active shipment per order, delivery status transitions are valid FSM)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["SapExportBatch (root: batchId, accumulates confirmed orders, tracks export status: pending → exported → acknowledged)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking (root: bookingId, maps shipmentId to carrier-specific tracking info, manages webhook event deduplication via carrier event IDs)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["No aggregates — stateless projector. Transforms and forwards events."]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes OrderConfirmed events. ERP Integration consumes and adapts to SAP format. Order Management is unaware of ERP needs."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier with ACL. Shipment Management publishes ShipmentCreated. Carrier Integration translates to DHL booking. Inbound: Carrier Integration acts as upstream for delivery status, feeding commands back into Shipment Management through an ACL."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Published Language. Both core contexts publish domain events in a canonical schema. Analytics Integration consumes all events via the published language and translates to analytics format."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Single Internal Event Bus as backbone rather than point-to-point integrations","gains":"Complete decoupling of integrations, easy to add new consumers, natural audit log of all events, independent scaling per consumer","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of running and monitoring a broker, eventual consistency between core and integrations, added latency (milliseconds) vs direct calls","alternatives_considered":["Point-to-point adapters called directly from domain service — simpler initially but creates tight coupling and N×M integration complexity","Shared database polling — avoids broker but introduces coupling through shared state and polling lag"]},{"decision":"Async webhook ingestion via queue rather than synchronous processing","gains":"Guaranteed sub-500ms webhook response regardless of processing complexity, retry semantics for free, dead-letter queue for poison messages","sacrifices":"Slight delay between webhook receipt and domain state update, additional infrastructure component (queue)","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous webhook processing — simpler but risks 500ms SLA breach under load or when core service is slow","Webhook processing with timeout-and-retry — fragile, DHL may not retry reliably"]},{"decision":"Batch XML generation for SAP on 15-minute schedule rather than real-time file drops","gains":"Matches SAP's consumption cadence exactly, reduces SFTP connection overhead, allows batch-level idempotency and error handling","sacrifices":"Up to 15-minute delay between order confirmation and SAP awareness, batch failures affect multiple orders","alternatives_considered":["Real-time individual XML file per order — excessive SFTP overhead, SAP may not process fast enough","SAP API integration instead of SFTP — would require SAP to expose APIs, unlikely given the SFTP requirement"]},{"decision":"Separate internal event bus topics from external analytics Kafka topics","gains":"Internal schema can evolve freely, analytics schema is a separate contract, security boundary between internal and external Kafka","sacrifices":"Duplication of event data across two Kafka clusters/topic sets, analytics projector is an additional moving part","alternatives_considered":["Direct consumption by analytics platform from internal topics — simpler but leaks internal schema and creates tight coupling, security risk of external system reading internal bus"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new integrations (System D, E, ...)","evolution_path":"New integrations only need a new consumer on the Internal Event Bus and a new ACL adapter. Zero changes to the core domain or existing adapters."},{"area":"Changing carrier from DHL to FedEx/UPS","evolution_path":"Implement a new Carrier Adapter behind the same ACL interface. The Carrier Integration bounded context abstracts carrier-specific details. Core domain emits the same ShipmentCreated events regardless of carrier."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Versioned events (v1, v2) with schema registry allow backward-compatible changes. New fields can be added without breaking existing consumers. Breaking changes use new event versions with a migration window."},{"area":"SAP replacement or modernization","evolution_path":"If SAP is replaced with a modern ERP with REST APIs, only the SAP Adapter changes — swap SFTP/XML for REST/JSON. The OrderConfirmed subscription and ACL boundary remain."},{"area":"Scaling webhook processing independently","evolution_path":"The DHL Webhook Ingestion Queue allows scaling webhook consumers horizontally independent of the webhook endpoint and the core domain service."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema is the central contract","reason":"All three integrations depend on the canonical domain event schema. Changes to event structure require coordinating across adapters. This is intentional rigidity — the canonical model should be stable and well-governed."},{"area":"Event ordering per aggregate","reason":"Internal Event Bus must guarantee per-orderId ordering. This constrains partitioning strategy and broker choice. Relaxing this would risk out-of-order processing (e.g., DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated)."},{"area":"Webhook endpoint availability","reason":"DHL will send webhooks regardless of our readiness. The endpoint must be highly available — it is a synchronous contract with an external system. Downtime means lost delivery status updates unless DHL retries."}],"risks":[{"risk":"DHL webhook delivery failures or retries with duplicate events could cause double-processing of delivery status updates","severity":"high","mitigation":"Idempotency keys derived from DHL's native event IDs stored in CarrierBooking aggregate. Deduplication at the ingestion queue consumer level. Dead-letter queue for unprocessable webhooks."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP batch failure (network, auth, disk full) causes a backlog of undelivered orders to ERP","severity":"high","mitigation":"SapExportBatch aggregate tracks export status. Failed batches are retried with exponential backoff. Alert on consecutive failures. Manual re-export capability via admin tooling."},{"risk":"Internal Event Bus outage halts all three integrations simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy event bus as a highly available cluster (e.g., Kafka with replication factor 3, min ISR 2). Monitor broker health aggressively. Consider a local outbox table in the core database as a fallback — events are first written to the outbox transactionally, then relayed to the bus (Transactional Outbox pattern)."},{"risk":"Analytics Kafka producer lag causes backpressure or data staleness in real-time analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer lag. Analytics projector runs in its own consumer group with independent scaling. Backpressure does not affect other integrations due to consumer group isolation."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal domain events and external system expectations","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward or full compatibility mode). CI pipeline validates that adapter transformations handle current event schema versions. Contract tests per adapter."},{"risk":"DHL API outage prevents shipment booking, blocking the fulfillment flow","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker on DHL API calls. Failed bookings are queued for retry (ShipmentCreated events remain on the bus with offset uncommitted or retried via a retry topic). Alert operations team for manual intervention if circuit stays open beyond threshold."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement incrementally, one integration at a time. Start with the Internal Event Bus and Core Domain events. Then add the Analytics Projector (simplest — stateless, read-only). Then the SAP Adapter (outbound only, batch schedule). Finally the DHL Adapter (most complex — bidirectional, real-time, webhook SLA). Each integration is independently deployable and testable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/core/domain/events/ — OrderPlaced.ts, OrderConfirmed.ts, ShipmentCreated.ts, DeliveryAttempted.ts, DeliveryCompleted.ts, DeliveryFailed.ts","src/core/domain/aggregates/ — Order.ts, Shipment.ts","src/infrastructure/eventbus/ — EventBusProducer.ts, EventBusConsumer.ts, EventSerializer.ts","src/integrations/sap/ — SapAdapter.ts, SapXmlMapper.ts, SftpClient.ts, SapExportBatchRepository.ts, SapBatchScheduler.ts","src/integrations/dhl/ — DhlAdapter.ts, DhlApiClient.ts, DhlDtoMapper.ts, DhlWebhookController.ts, DhlWebhookConsumer.ts, CarrierBookingRepository.ts","src/integrations/analytics/ — AnalyticsProjector.ts, AnalyticsEventMapper.ts, ExternalKafkaProducer.ts","src/infrastructure/monitoring/ — IntegrationHealthMonitor.ts","tests/integration/ — sap-adapter.integration.test.ts, dhl-adapter.integration.test.ts, analytics-projector.integration.test.ts","tests/contract/ — dhl-webhook.contract.test.ts, sap-xml.contract.test.ts, analytics-schema.contract.test.ts"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters) — core domain defines ports (event publisher interface, carrier booking port, ERP export port).
